regimeSub: Iran war de-escalates and Hormuz reopens, collapsing the oil shock just as Warsh's Fed pivots hawkish and the AI trade cracks.
themes: Hormuz reopens -> Brent back to $76 -> energy-driven CPI spike set to fade through H2
Warsh Fed kills cutting bias -> September HIKE risk live -> front-end stays anchored, real rates grind higher
AI euphoria unwinds -> SOX and megacap tech roll over -> SPX vulnerable despite oil relief
Sticky 4.2% CPI + 1.6% GDP -> stagflation-lite -> Fed boxed between price stability and slowing growth

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy 3.63% vs 4.2% CPI = negative real, but Warsh wants it positive; 10y real ~+0.3% and rising.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +27bp, modestly positive; hike rhetoric caps steepening, bear-flatten risk if Fed delivers.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Still contained as oil falls, but AI tech unwind is the credit tail to watch.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 19.5 — elevated but not panic; AI selloff and Fed hawkishness keep a bid under vol.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $76 and sliding as Hormuz reopens; Macquarie pulled $200 call — war premium has fully bled out.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $4075 holds near highs despite oil drop — hedging Fed credibility and fiscal/inflation tail, not war.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1537 weak won, but KOSPI +3% on chip dip-buying; FX vulnerable to SOX swings and BoK.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: Epicenter of the selloff; AI fears deepen, Micron earnings the next binary catalyst.

headline: The Iran war is ending and oil is deflating, but a hawkish Warsh Fed and a cracking AI trade have replaced the energy shock as the market's dominant fear.
